channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The ‘Super Mario Galaxy’ Movie Did Rosalina Dirty
item.description: Brie Larson's take on the beloved 'Galaxy' games' princess was meant to be one of the highlights of the film—instead, it was one of its biggest fumbles.

item.title: Google quietly releases an offline-first AI dictation app on iOS
item.description: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

item.title: The best deals on MacBooks right now
item.description: It’s 2026, and that means Apple sells MacBooks equipped with not just its capable M-series processors but A-series chips that were previously exclusive to the iPhone. With the MacBook Neo starting at $599, the company now offers a wider range of options with more sizes and price points than ever before, all of which run […]

item.title: DJI’s Mic Mini records clear audio on the go, and it’s on sale for $60
item.description: If you regularly create content for TikTok, Instagram, or YouTube on the go, bad sound quality can easily take away from an otherwise great video. DJI’s Mic Mini helps you record clear, reliable sound for videos without carrying around bulky, expensive gear, and it’s currently down to its all-time low price. You can buy the […]

item.title: Netflix launches a standalone app for kids’ games
item.description: Netflix says the app gives children access to an "ever-growing" library of games for kids.

item.title: Why safety regulators closed their investigation into Tesla’s remote parking feature
item.description: NHTSA closed its investigation into Tesla's "Actually Smart Summon" feature, saying that only a fraction of cases resulted in an incident, and that no incidents resulted in injury. Tesla has also issued a number of software updates.

item.title: Border Patrol Agents Sold Challenge Coins With ‘Charlotte’s Web’ Characters in Riot Gear
item.description: Nonprofits run out of US Border Patrol stations are also selling other “operation”-themed coins that include a phrase popularized by the Proud Boys, potentially in violation of government rules.

item.title: Robotaxi companies won’t say how often remote operators intervene
item.description: Autonomous vehicle companies are refusing to disclose key details about their use of remote assistance teams, including how often these workers are forced to intervene to help their self-driving cars. Senator Ed Markey (D-MA) had asked robotaxi companies to disclose the information as part of an investigation by his office into the use of remote […]...

item.title: Three YouTubers accuse Apple of illegal scraping to train its AI models
item.description: Three YouTube channels have banded together and filed a class action lawsuit against Apple, as first spotted by MacRumors. According to the lawsuit, the creators behind h3h3 Productions, MrShortGameGolf and Golfholics have accused Apple of violating the Digital Millennium Copyright Act by scraping copyrighted videos on YouTube to train its AI models. While the YouTubers' videos are available to watch on the platform, the lawsuit alleged that Apple illegally circumvented the "controlled streaming architecture" that regular users are limited to. The creators claimed that Apple's video scraping w...

item.title: Wisconsin Remains a Gooning Sanctuary State After Governor Rejects Age Verification Bill
item.description: Digital ID requirements stop at the border.

item.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
item.description: Iran said it will target U.S.-linked data centers with new missile strikes, as the war between the U.S. and Iran escalates.

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew just flew farther away from Earth than anyone ever has before
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew just set a new distance record in miles traveled away from Earth. The team of four astronauts are in the process of circling the Moon, reaching 5,000 miles beyond the natural satellite. That brings the total distance traveled away from our home to over 250,000 miles. Gene Roddenberry would be proud. This broke the previous 1970 record set by Apollo 13's crew by around 4,000 miles. The four astronauts – Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover and Christina Koch of NASA and Jeremy Hansen of the Canadian Space Agency – are the first humans to cross the lunar threshold since 1972's Apol...

item.title: The Artemis 2 Astronauts Are Now Farther From Earth Than Any Have Gone Before
item.description: The crew just surpassed the record previously set by the Apollo 13 astronauts in 1970 for the farthest distance ever traveled from our home planet.

item.title: The Imperial Credits From the ‘Andor’ Aldhani Heist Are Up for Auction, Which We’re Sure Is Not a Commentary on Capitalism at All
item.description: Heritage Auctions' third annual 'Star Wars' Day event features several props from the acclaimed Disney+ series starring Diego Luna and Stellan Skarsgård.

item.title: Can Artificial Intelligence be governed—or will it govern us?
item.description: Now is the time to choose between recklessness and prudence On July 16th, 1945, when the world’s first nuclear explosion shook the plains of New Mexico, J. Robert Oppenheimer, who led the project, quoted the Bhagavad Gita, “Now I am become Death, the destroyer of worlds.” And indeed, he had. The world was never truly the same after nuclear power became a reality....

item.title: Test abxylute N6 : la manette pour les joueurs nomades qui savent se débrouiller
item.description: Parmi les nombreux accessoires qui gravitent autour de la Nintendo Switch 2, des projets tentent aussi de voir le jour autour d'un type de proposition. L'Abxylute N6 vise, elle, les joueurs nomades avec une manette faite pour jouer à la console de Nintendo en portable.

item.title: Microsoft TOS: Copilot is for entertainment purposes only, not important advice
item.description: Microsoft's terms of service for Copilot AI has some very surprising language about the AI chatbot.

item.title: Où et quand regarder le direct d’Artemis II qui survole la Lune ? YouTube, NASA+, Netflix, Prime Video et plus encore
item.description: Ce soir, la mission Artemis II passe au plus près de la surface lunaire. Entre les photos haute définition et les vidéos laser en 4K, la NASA a mis les petits plats dans les grands pour nous faire vivre l'événement. Voici les horaires précis pour la France métropolitaine.

item.title: Voici pourquoi il ne faut pas acheter l’Oppo Find N6
item.description: L'Oppo Find N6 est probablement le pliant le plus abouti qu'on ait eu entre les mains. Pliure quasi invisible, épaisseur ridicule, performances solides et photo Hasselblad convaincante. Seul problème : il ne sera pas commercialisé en France. Alors autant se persuader qu'on n'en a pas besoin.

item.title: Apple plans Supreme Court appeal in Epic Games App Store battle — again
item.description: Apple plans to ask the Supreme Court to review its App Store fight with Epic Games, as it challenges a ruling limiting its ability to charge fees on external payments.

item.title: I compared Artemis II mission's historic dark side of the moon photo with my Sony Alpha A6000, and the differences just blew me away
item.description: The Artemis II mission is starting to capture never-before-viewed (by human eyes) views of the moon, and this should excite stargazers and photographers.

item.title: Check Your Medicine Cabinet for These Eye Drop Bottles After Millions Recalled
item.description: The eye drops are sold at Rite-Aid, Walgreens, CVS, and more.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts are sending back more stunning photos of Earth
item.description: The historic Artemis II crew is on a 10-day voyage around the moon, and they're sending back beautiful pictures of planet Earth.

item.title: Artemis 2 Astronauts’ Epic iPhone Photos Are All… Selfies?
item.description: Turns out the incredible photos taken by the astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft are from the iPhone 17 Pro Max's front-facing camera and not its better rear shooters.

item.title: Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire proposal as Trump’s deadline nears
item.description: Trump has warned Iran that the U.S. could set the country ‘back to the stone ages.’ Iran on Monday rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal and said it wants a permanent end to the war, even as Israel attacked a major gas field and U.S. President Donald Trump’s ultimatum to open the Strait of Hormuz loomed.

item.title: Pourquoi Google sort-il ses meilleures pépites IA sur iPhone ? Voici de la dictée vocale hors-ligne et gratuite
item.description: Google vient de sortir une application assez mystérieuse sur iOS : Google AI Edge Eloquent. Cette application promet de transformer vos notes vocales brouillonnes en textes impeccables, le tout sans abonnement et potentiellement sans connexion.

item.title: Il aspire tout, sauf vos économies : ce robot aspirateur-laveur Xiaomi avec sa station complète ne dépasse pas les 500 €
item.description: Dévoilé en 2025, le Xiaomi Robot Vacuum 5 est un aspirateur robot qui présente un très bon rapport qualité/prix. Actuellement, la marque le propose à 499,99 euros au lieu de 649,99 euros.

item.title: The day Earth slipped behind the moon: the Artemis II crews eclipse
item.description: The Artemis II mission's close lunar flyby brings Earthset, a rare long solar eclipse, and Earthrise for the astronauts on April 6.

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II mission to fly around the far side of the Moon
item.description: Humans haven’t stepped foot on the Moon since NASA’s Apollo 17 mission in 1972. Now, the space agency is racing to get back to the lunar surface under the umbrella of its Artemis program — a nod to the Greek goddess and twin sister of Apollo, whose name was given to NASA’s first program to […]

item.title: JetBlue’s new credit card perks aim to compete with Amex and Chase
item.description: The airline is adding companion passes, travel credits, and loyalty perks as competition among premium cards intensifies. JetBlue announced Monday plans to give its top-tier credit card a refresh, adding new travel credits, companion perks, and loyalty boosts as airlines and issuers keep escalating what “premium” actually means.
